import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  mut i = 0
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut current = 0
  mut sign = 1
  mut has_digit = false
  while i < n
    match string.byte_at(s, i)
    when Some(b)
      if b == 32 or b == 9 or b == 13
        if has_digit
          result = list.push(result, current * sign)
          current = 0
          sign = 1
          has_digit = false
        end
      else if b == 45 and not has_digit
        sign = -1
      else if b >= 48 and b <= 57
        current = current * 10 + (b - 48)
        has_digit = true
      end
    when None
      ()
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if has_digit
    result = list.push(result, current * sign)
  end
  result
end

fn find_pair(_ nums: List<Int>, _ t: Int) -> Option<Int>
  let n = list.len(nums)
  mut i = 0
  mut result: Option<Int> = None
  while i < n
    mut j = i + 1
    while j < n
      match list.get(nums, i)
      when Some(a)
        match list.get(nums, j)
        when Some(b)
          if a + b == t
            match result
            when None
              result = Some(i * 1000000 + j)
            when Some(_)
              ()
            end
          end
        when None
          ()
        end
      when None
        ()
      end
      j = j + 1
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  result
end

match io.read_line()
when Some(line1)
  match io.read_line()
  when Some(line2)
    let nums = parse_ints(line1)
    let targets = parse_ints(line2)
    match list.get(targets, 0)
    when Some(t)
      match find_pair(nums, t)
      when Some(packed)
        let i = packed / 1000000
        let j = packed - i * 1000000
        println("found=#{i},#{j}")
      when None
        println("none")
      end
    when None
      println("none")
    end
  when None
    println("none")
  end
when None
  println("none")
end
